name: Chocolate-Almond Sandwiches
recipeInstructions:
White Chocolate Ganache:
1. Grind almonds and sugar in a food processor until fine.
2. Sift flour, cocoa powder, and baking powder into a medium bowl.
3. Cream butter in a large bowl.
4. Add almond mixture; beat until smooth and fluffy.
5. Stir in cocoa mixture with a wooden spoon until dough is smooth.
6. Divide dough in half and shape into logs about 1.5 inches in diameter.
7. Wrap logs in waxed paper and refrigerate for 2 hours.
8. Roll each log to an even round shape, refrigerating for another 4 hours or until firm.
9. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
10. Cut logs into about 1/4 inch thick slices with a sharp knife.
11. Place slices on ungreased baking sheets about 1.5 inches apart.
12. Bake for 10-11 minutes or until set, being careful not to burn on the bottom.
13. Transfer to racks and cool completely.
White Chocolate Ganache:
1. Put white chocolate in a small bowl.
2. Bring whipping cream to a full boil in a small saucepan.
3. Pour cream over chocolate all at once and stir with a whisk until smooth.
4. Cool mixture to room temperature.
5. Refrigerate for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally until cold and thick but not set.
6. Whip the ganache at high speed for 3 minutes or until lighter in color and thickened.
7. Spread about 2 teaspoons of ganache on flat sides of half the cookies.
8. Sandwich each with a flat side of a second cookie, using cookies of the same size.
9. Refrigerate sandwiches for 15 minutes to set before serving.
These cocoa-almond cookies can be kept covered in the refrigerator for up to 1 week and are best served at room temperature. Makes 28 sandwich cookies.
